title.rendered: How Much Does Colouring Your Hair Cost

content.rendered:
Colouring your hair is one of the fastest ways to refresh your look. Prices vary because every head of hair has different needs, goals, and starting points. This guide shows what affects the cost, what you can expect to pay, and why a proper consultation at Groove Unisex Salon ensures you get the right result for your hair type and style.

Groove Unisex Salon offers customized colour services based on your hair length, thickness, and the finish you want. We also offer a full range of women’s hair services that pair well with colour appointments, especially for clients who want a complete refresh.

Skilled stylists review your hair condition, choose the right product line, and recommend a colour plan that gives long-lasting shine and strength.

What Affects the Cost of Hair Colouring?

Understanding what shapes the final price helps you plan your visit better. Colour work is not a one-size service; your hair history and colour goals influence everything from product usage to timing.

1. Hair Length and Thickness

Long or thick hair uses more colour and takes more time to process. This increases both product use and application time, which raises the final cost. Shorter or finer hair usually falls at the lower end of the price range.

2. Current Hair Condition

If your hair has previous colour, dryness, or breakage, your stylist may recommend strengthening treatments such as moisture or bond repair. This keeps your hair healthy during the colouring process and helps the shade last longer.

Related Article: SOS for Damaged Hair: Spa Treatments That Actually Work

3. The Type of Colour Service

Each service requires a different level of detail. A full colour is direct and even, while highlights or balayage involve more steps and precision work. More steps require more expertise and time.

4. Desired Lightness or Depth

Lifting dark hair to a light blonde usually needs several stages to protect the hair. Adding depth or subtle tone shifts often needs less time and product.

5. Level of Maintenance You Want

Some shades require regular touch-ups. Others grow out softly and stretch your appointment timeline. During your consultation, your stylist can help you choose a colour that matches the level of upkeep you prefer.

Typical Price Ranges for Popular Colour Services

Pricing varies across salons in Ontario, but these ranges give you a clear idea of what to expect when you book a colour service at a professional studio.

Full Colour

A full colour applies one solid shade across your hair.

Average cost: $90 to $180

This is ideal for refreshing your natural shade, covering greys, or shifting to a warmer or cooler tone.

Partial Highlights

Partial highlights brighten the top and sides for a natural lift.

  • Average cost: $120 to $200

They blend well with most haircuts and suit clients who want brightness with low upkeep.

Full Highlights

Full highlights add brightness throughout the entire head.

  • Average cost: $160 to $260

This service involves more foils and a longer processing time.

Balayage

Balayage creates soft, blended colour that grows out gently.

  • Average cost: $180 to $300

This method takes more time because it uses freehand painting for seamless colour shifts.

Colour Correction

Correcting banding, uneven tone, or at-home colour mishaps often requires several stages.

  • Average cost: $250 to $450+

Your stylist maps out a step-by-step plan to protect your hair and restore a balanced shade.

Gloss or Toner

A toner refreshes colour, removes brassiness, and adds shine.

  • Average cost: $25 to $60

It is often used between appointments to keep the colour bright.

Related Article: Balayage vs. Ombre: Which Trendy Look to Choose for This Summer

Why Colour Prices Vary From Client to Client

Even when two clients ask for the same colour, their hair may react differently. This is why a consultation is essential. At Groove Unisex Salon, stylists review your hair texture, previous treatments, and your colour history to pick the right product blend and technique.

This personalized approach avoids damage, improves colour longevity, and supports overall hair health.

Common factors that can change the final price:

  • Your natural base shade
  • Whether you have box dye on your hair
  • Whether you need extra product
  • If you need toning or bond repair
  • If more than one lightning session is required

Transparent pricing is always discussed before your service begins, so you can make informed decisions.

Should You Add a Hair Treatment?

A treatment can be paired with any colour service to keep your hair strong. Groove Unisex Salon offers conditioning, moisture repair, and bond-building options that boost shine and resilience.

Adding a treatment is recommended if you colour often, use heat tools daily, or want long-lasting smoothness.

Related Article: How to Choose the Right Hair Colour for Your Skin Tone

How to Get the Best Value from Your Hair Colour Appointment

Colouring your hair is an investment. You can extend the life of your shade and reduce future costs with simple habits.

  1. Choose shades that match your lifestyle: If you prefer fewer salon visits, blended balayage or soft highlights offer longer grow-out periods.
  2. Follow aftercare advice: Your stylist will suggest shampoos, conditioners, and treatments suited to your colour. This keeps fading low and shining high.
  3. Book refresh appointments at the right time: Most colours look their best when refreshed every 6 to 10 weeks. Balayage can stretch up to 12 to 16 weeks.
  4. Stay consistent with your stylist: Keeping a record of your hair history helps your stylist plan future sessions with precision.

What This Means for Your Hair Colour Budget

Hair colouring costs depend on your hair length, condition, and the technique used. A consultation at Groove Unisex Salon gives you an accurate quote, a colour plan that fits your upkeep level, and professional guidance that protects your hair health.

Skilled stylists create shades that match your features and give you a smooth grow-out pattern. Book your colour appointment to get a result that feels fresh, bright, and easy to maintain.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does a colour appointment take?

Most colour services take 2 to 4 hours. Complex work, such as balayage or correction, may need more time because it involves several steps to protect your hair.

Can I colour my hair if it’s dry or damaged?

Yes, but your stylist may recommend a repair treatment during the appointment. This helps reduce breakage and allows colour to process evenly.

How often should I refresh my colour?

Solid colours and highlights usually need touch-ups every 6 to 10 weeks. Balayage can stretch to several months with proper care.

Will my hair get damaged from colouring?

Professional colour is safer than at-home box dye because your stylist balances developer strength and product choice based on your hair condition.

What’s the difference between highlights and balayage?

Highlights use foils for noticeable brightness. Balayage uses freehand painting for soft, natural blends with longer grow-out.
